author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ore mana! ki rase haiyā raili bhora
ki baliyā eli sethā, ki kāja vā kara hethā,
tileka cetana nāhi tora

putra dārā⋅sampada, jīvana⋅yauvana⋅mada,
je kara, sakali asāra
jalabimba katakṣaṇa, temati jāniha mana,
tribhuvane ‘kṛṣṇa’ mātra sāra

je dina je gela jāya, jā āche sāmālo tāya,
kāladūta dā̐ḍāiyā pathe
chāḍiyā anyathā kāma, bala ‘rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa’ nāma,
kabhu dekhā nā habe tā⋅sāthe

ājñākārī brahmā⋅hara, śamana⋅kiṅkara āra,
sura⋅muni je pada dheyāya
hena kṛṣṇa-pada chāḍi’, gale diyā māyādaḍi,
duḥkha deha kena re āmāya

premānanda kahe bhāi, ‘kṛṣṇa’ vinā gati nāi,
bhaja kṛṣṇa⋅caraṇāravinde
saṁsāra⋅sāgare paḍi’, kena kara kāḍubāḍi,
kaha ‘kṛṣṇa’ taribe ānande
en_translation:
O my mind! In what pleasure have you remained immersed? For what reason did you come to this world, and what work are you doing here? You do not have even a moment of awareness.

Children, family, wealth, life, and the pride of youth—whatever you pursue, all of it is worthless. How long does a bubble on the water last? Know that everything is like that. In the three worlds, only Lord Kṛṣṇa is the true essence.

Whatever days have passed are gone; take care of what remains. The messenger of death is standing on the path. Abandoning all other desires, chant the Names of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, and then you will never have to meet that messenger.

Lord Brahmā and Lord Śiva are obedient to Him, as are the servants of death. The demigods and sages meditate upon His feet. Abandoning such lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa and putting the rope of illusion around your neck, why do you give me so much sorrow?

Premānanda says: "O brother! Without Lord Kṛṣṇa there is no refuge. Worship the lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa. Having fallen into the ocean of material existence, why do you make such a fuss? Chant 'Kṛṣṇa!' and you will cross over with joy."
